Title: A systems genetics approach reveals PbrNSC as a regulator of lignin and cellulose biosynthesis in stone cells of pear fruit

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAC transcription factor; secondary cell wall NAC subgroup; contains LP-box and WQ-box C-terminal activation motifs
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Secondary cell wall formation and thickening; contributes to stone cell formation in pear fruit and mechanical support in vascular tissues
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Stone cells of pear fruit flesh; interfascicular fiber and xylem tissues of Arabidopsis inflorescence stems
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes lignin and cellulose accumulation and stone cell formation; overexpression increases lignin/cellulose and SCW thickening, while silencing reduces them
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Secondary cell wall biosynthesis; lignin biosynthesis; cellulose biosynthesis; xylan-associated SCW program
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Systems genetics integrating co-expression network and eQTL mapping; correlation with traits; transient overexpression and VIGS silencing in pear fruit; stable overexpression in Arabidopsis; complementation of Arabidopsis nst1/nst3 mutant; dual-luciferase transactivation assay; qRT-PCR; lignin/cellulose quantification; histochemical staining; TEM
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PbrMYB169; Pbr4CL4; PbrLAC4; PbrLAC5; PbrCESA4a; PbrCESA7a; PbrCESA8b
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates transcription of PbrMYB169, Pbr4CL4, and PbrLAC4 directly in dual-luciferase assays; positively regulates expression of multiple lignin, cellulose, and xylan biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Developmental stage of early fruit development; stone cell-forming tissues; NA for direct upstream regulator
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Abundant in stems and young fruits (21–63 DAFB); low in anthers, leaves, and older fruits; higher in isolated stone cells than in flesh tissue
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In pear, abundant in stems and young fruits and enriched in stone cells; in Arabidopsis, transgene driven by CaMV 35S or AtNST3 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive regulator identified by integrated co-expression/eQTL analysis; located in an eQTL hotspot and linked to both lignin and cellulose genes, making it a decisive controller of stone cell lignocellulose deposition
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage (notably 35–63 DAFB in pear; assays at 35 DAFB infiltration and 49 DAFB population profiling); greenhouse growth at 22°C/16-h photoperiod for Arabidopsis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation via promoter binding; LP-box and WQ-box are required for full transactivation activity
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter; Arabidopsis AtNST3 promoter (3.0 kb)
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In Arabidopsis, ectopic expression caused unconventional lignified SCW deposition in epidermal cells, indicating possible risk of ectopic wall thickening or texture penalties in engineering contexts
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of 206 pear cultivars and developmental series; eQTL mapping; co-expression network analysis; SRA: PRJNA723405
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to reduce pear stone cell content and improve fruit texture through allele selection, marker-assisted breeding, or genome editing/downregulation; nonsynonymous SNP Chr6_6,385,286 (Ser227Pro) proposed as potential breeding marker; caution needed because strong ectopic expression may induce undesirable SCW deposition
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PbrNSC was identified by integrating stone cell module eQTLs with a lignocellulose co-expression network as a candidate regulator of pear stone cell formation. 2. PbrNSC expression positively correlates with stone cell, lignin, and cellulose contents and is enriched in young fruits and isolated stone cells. 3. Transient overexpression in pear fruit increased lignin and cellulose, whereas silencing decreased both. 4. Heterologous expression in Arabidopsis increased SCW-related gene expression, lignin/cellulose accumulation, and SCW thickness. 5. PbrNSC directly activated promoters of PbrMYB169, Pbr4CL4, and PbrLAC4, and its LP-box and WQ-box were required for transcriptional activation and functional complementation of nst1/nst3.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PbrNSC was named NAC STONE CELL PROMOTING FACTOR. It is the only SCW-clade NAC abundantly expressed during early pear fruit development. A nonsynonymous SNP at Chr6_6,385,286 in the PbrNSC locus is associated with stone cell and lignin variation. The paper supports functional equivalence to Arabidopsis NST3 in SCW regulation.
